data.title: Does LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis (BVMF:LPSB3) Deserve A Spot On Your Watchlist?
data.description:
It's common for many investors, especially those who are inexperienced, to buy shares in companies with a good story even if these companies are loss-making. But the reality is that when a company loses money each year, for long enough, its investors will usually take their share of those losses. Loss-making companies are always racing against time to reach financial sustainability, so investors in these companies may be taking on more risk than they should.

Despite being in the age of tech-stock blue-sky investing, many investors still adopt a more traditional strategy; buying shares in profitable companies like LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis (BVMF:LPSB3). Now this is not to say that the company presents the best investment opportunity around, but profitability is a key component to success in business.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis' Earnings Per Share Are Growing

If a company can keep growing earnings per share (EPS) long enough, its share price should eventually follow. So it makes sense that experienced investors pay close attention to company EPS when undertaking investment research. Recognition must be given to the that LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis has grown EPS by 55% per year, over the last three years. Growth that fast may well be fleeting, but it should be more than enough to pique the interest of the wary stock pickers.

One way to double-check a company's growth is to look at how its revenue, and earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) margins are changing. LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis maintained stable EBIT margins over the last year, all while growing revenue 5.6% to R$203m. That's encouraging news for the company!

In the chart below, you can see how the company has grown earnings and revenue, over time. To see the actual numbers, click on the chart.

See our latest analysis for LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis

Since LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis is no giant, with a market capitalisation of R$242m, you should definitely check its cash and debt before getting too excited about its prospects.

Are LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis Insiders Aligned With All Shareholders?

Theory would suggest that it's an encouraging sign to see high insider ownership of a company, since it ties company performance directly to the financial success of its management. So as you can imagine, the fact that LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis insiders own a significant number of shares certainly is appealing. Owning 50% of the company, insiders have plenty riding on the performance of the the share price. This should be a welcoming sign for investors because it suggests that the people making the decisions are also impacted by their choices. In terms of absolute value, insiders have R$120m invested in the business, at the current share price. That's nothing to sneeze at!

Is LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis Worth Keeping An Eye On?

LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis' earnings per share growth have been climbing higher at an appreciable rate. That EPS growth certainly is attention grabbing, and the large insider ownership only serves to further stoke our interest. At times fast EPS growth is a sign the business has reached an inflection point, so there's a potential opportunity to be had here. Based on the sum of its parts, we definitely think its worth watching LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis very closely. We should say that we've discovered 3 warning signs for LPS Brasil - Consultoria de Imóveis that you should be aware of before investing here.

There's always the possibility of doing well buying stocks that are not growing earnings and do not have insiders buying shares. But for those who consider these important metrics, we encourage you to check out companies that do have those features. You can access a tailored list of Brazilian companies which have demonstrated growth backed by significant insider holdings.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider ING Groep (ENXTAM:INGA) After A 71% One Year Rally?
data.description:
• Investors may be wondering if ING Groep at €24.20 is still offering value after a strong run, or if most of the easy gains are already on the table.
  • The stock has returned 4.2% over the last 7 days and 7.3% over the last 30 days, with a 1 year return of 71.3%, 3 year return of 159.0% and 5 year return of 226.2%. The year to date return currently stands at a 1.2% decline.
  • Recent coverage has focused on ING Groep's share price strength over the past year and how it compares with the broader Banks sector and wider market. Other news has highlighted ongoing investor interest in European banking stocks, which provides context for the recent moves in ING Groep's share price.
  • Right now, ING Groep has a valuation score of 5 out of 6. The rest of this article will compare different valuation approaches to explore what that might mean for the current price, before finishing with a way to look at valuation that goes beyond the usual ratios.

ING Groep delivered 71.3% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Banks industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: ING Groep Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how efficiently ING Groep is using shareholder capital, comparing the returns it generates to the cost of that capital. Instead of focusing on cash flows, it focuses on earnings and book value to estimate what each share could be worth if those economics stay broadly similar over time.

For ING Groep, the current Book Value is €18.63 per share and the Stable Book Value estimate is €18.68 per share, based on weighted future Book Value estimates from 11 analysts. Stable EPS is €2.63 per share, sourced from weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 15 analysts. Against a Cost of Equity of €1.17 per share, this implies an Excess Return of €1.45 per share and an Average Return on Equity of 14.06%.

Feeding these inputs into the Excess Returns model produces an estimated intrinsic value of about €52.47 per share. Compared with the current share price of €24.20, this suggests the stock is 53.9% undervalued according to this approach.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests ING Groep is undervalued by 53.9%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 239 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for ING Groep.

Approach 2: ING Groep Price vs Earnings

For a profitable bank like ING Groep, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to link what you pay today with the earnings the business is generating. It helps you see how many euros investors are currently willing to pay for each euro of profit.

What counts as a "normal" or "fair" P/E depends on how fast earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings are. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk usually support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk often go with a lower P/E.

ING Groep currently trades on a P/E of 8.35x. That sits below the Banks industry average P/E of 11.14x and below the broader peer average of 13.81x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 8.84x for ING Groep, which is the P/E level suggested after accounting for factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, risks, industry and market cap. This Fair Ratio goes a step further than simple industry or peer comparisons because it adjusts for ING Groep’s specific profile rather than assuming all banks should trade on the same multiple.

Since the current P/E of 8.35x is below the Fair Ratio of 8.84x, the shares look undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your ING Groep Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives on Simply Wall St’s Community page give you a simple story behind your numbers, letting you link your view of ING Groep’s business to a forecast for revenue, earnings and margins, and then to a Fair Value you can compare with the current price. Each Narrative updates automatically as news or results arrive. This is why one investor might build a Narrative that ING Groep is worth €32.20 based on stronger fee income and digital growth, while another might see a Fair Value closer to €18.23 if they focus more on capital demands and margin pressure.

For ING Groep however we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading ING Groep Narratives:

🐂 ING Groep Bull Case

Implied discount vs current price: 13.35% undervalued

Revenue growth assumption: 9%

  • Sees EU infrastructure spending as a key support for loan demand and net interest income over time.
  • Emphasises ING Groep's shift toward fee based income such as wealth management, M&A and underwriting to reduce reliance on interest margins.
  • Argues that, despite prior share price gains and dividend payments, there is still room for further upside once risks are accounted for.

  • Highlights risks around funding costs, competition, and execution on cost savings and technology investments that could pressure margins.
  • Aligns with a more cautious analyst cohort that sets a lower Fair Value, with the view that the current share price may already reflect optimistic expectations.

data.title: SAN Altech TTM Margin Slip To 4.3% Tests Bullish Quality Earnings Narrative
data.description:
Sankyo Altech (TSE:2659) has wrapped up FY 2026 with fourth quarter revenue of ¥78,029 million and basic EPS of ¥42.06, while trailing 12 month revenue stands at ¥245,547 million with EPS of ¥172.66 as investors weigh these outcomes against a current share price of ¥3,355. Over recent periods the company has reported quarterly revenue in a range from ¥54,358 million to ¥78,029 million, with basic EPS between ¥35.62 and ¥53.74 against a trailing 12 month net income of ¥10,678 million, offering a clear indication of its scale and earnings power. With net profit margins at 4.3% over the last year versus 4.8% previously, the latest figures keep attention on how efficiently that revenue is being converted into profit.

See our full analysis for SAN-ALTD.

With the headline numbers in place, the next step is to see how SAN-ALTD's results line up with the widely followed narratives around its growth profile, income appeal, and valuation premium.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Margins Ease Back From 4.8% To 4.3%

  • Trailing 12 month net profit came in at ¥10,678 million on revenue of ¥245,547 million, which works out to a 4.3% margin compared with 4.8% the previous year.
  • What stands out for a bearish view is that this lower 4.3% margin sits alongside five year earnings growth of 14.9% per year. Critics highlight that the recent margin slip contrasts with that growth record and
    • point to the most recent year of negative earnings growth as a sign that the longer term trend is not reflected in the latest numbers,
    • and argue that if margins stayed closer to the earlier 4.8% level, net profit on ¥245,547 million of revenue would look stronger than the ¥10,678 million actually reported.
Stay curious about how margin shifts like this can matter more than headline revenue when you think about profit resilience for a retailer, then dig into how bears frame that risk in the 🐻 SAN-ALTD Bear Case.

Five Year EPS Growth Versus Recent TTM Drift

  • Over the last five years, earnings are reported to have grown 14.9% per year, while trailing 12 month EPS of ¥172.66 sits below the recent trailing peak of ¥187.95 seen in early FY 2026.
  • Supporters of a bullish angle focus on that 14.9% annual earnings growth and describe past earnings as high quality. The data partly backs them yet also adds friction because
    • quarterly EPS during FY 2026 ranged from ¥35.62 to ¥47.73, which is a relatively tight band that fits a steady growth story,
    • but the slip in trailing EPS from ¥187.95 to ¥172.66 over recent quarters shows that even a long run growth rate can be paired with a softer near term trend that bulls need to factor in.
If you want to see how bullish investors connect this earnings history to their long term story on SAN-ALTD, check out the 🐂 SAN-ALTD Bull Case.

Rich 19.4x P/E With 2.98% Yield

  • At a share price of ¥3,355, the stock trades on a 19.4x P/E, above the JP Consumer Retailing industry average of 14x, while offering a 2.98% dividend yield and sitting above a DCF fair value of ¥2,887.89.
  • Analysts who see a tension between valuation and fundamentals point out that the 19.4x P/E and price above DCF fair value sit alongside forecast earnings growth of about 8.05% per year and revenue growth of 3.5% per year
    • which are both below the JP market forecasts of 10.1% earnings growth and 5.6% revenue growth,
    • yet the stock is still priced at a premium multiple, so investors are left weighing that slower forecast growth against the combination of a near 3% yield and a history of high quality past earnings.

Next Steps

• In early April 2026, DXC Technology announced a new multi-year agreement with ServiceNow to act as Customer Zero for ServiceNow’s Core Business Suite, deploying agentic AI across its Global Business Services model to modernize complex, multivendor enterprise operations.
  • This move effectively turns DXC into a large-scale proving ground for ServiceNow’s AI platform, generating validated, reusable automation blueprints and industry use cases that can be rolled out to other enterprises.
  • Next, we’ll examine how DXC’s Customer Zero role for ServiceNow’s agentic AI platform could influence the company’s AI-led investment narrative.

ServiceNow Investment Narrative Recap

To own ServiceNow, you need to believe that its AI platform can stay central to large enterprises as workflows, data and compliance get more complex, and that the company can justify a premium valuation with solid execution. Right now, the key near term catalyst is converting AI interest into meaningful usage and revenue, while the biggest risk is execution around AI pricing and adoption in a softer software tape. The DXC “Customer Zero” news is directionally positive but not a clear near term game changer on its own.

Among recent developments, the US$3.0 billion revolving credit facility announced on April 1, 2026 stands out. It gives ServiceNow extra financial flexibility just as it leans harder into AI agents, acquisitions and consumption based models, all of which can be capital intensive and volatile in payback. For investors focused on catalysts, that liquidity backstop may matter if AI investments or federal spending headwinds create more lumpiness in results or cash flow.

Yet investors should also be aware that the real pressure point could be how quickly customers adapt to ServiceNow’s evolving AI pricing model and what happens if...

data.title: Is Visteon (VC) Still Attractive After A 33% One Year Share Price Gain
data.description:

  • Recent coverage of Visteon has focused on its position in the auto components space and how investors are reassessing companies tied to vehicle production and technology. This context helps explain why sentiment around the stock can shift as investors weigh its role in the sector against broader market conditions.

Approach 1: Visteon Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of the cash Visteon could generate in the future and discounts those amounts back to today, to arrive at an estimated value per share in today’s dollars.

For Visteon, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flows in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is reported at $281.56 million. Analysts provide explicit free cash flow estimates out to 2030, for example $240.40 million in 2030, and Simply Wall St extrapolates further projections from there using the same framework.

After discounting these projected cash flows, the DCF model indicates an estimated intrinsic value of about $154.37 per share, compared with the current share price of around $97. This implies a 37.2% discount to the model’s estimate. This suggests the shares may be trading below this DCF based value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Visteon Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a handy shorthand for how much you are paying for each dollar of current earnings, which makes it a useful cross check against the DCF result you saw earlier.

What counts as a "normal" P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower, more cautious multiple.

Visteon currently trades on a P/E of 12.95x. That sits below both the Auto Components industry average of 16.32x and the peer group average of 16.21x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 12.89x for Visteon. This Fair Ratio reflects factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and company specific risks, rather than just lining it up against broad industry or peer averages.

Because the Fair Ratio is tailored to Visteon’s own profile, it can be a more precise guide than simple peer comparisons. With the actual P/E of 12.95x sitting very close to the Fair Ratio of 12.89x, the shares look priced at about the level this framework would suggest.

data.title: Freshpet (FRPT) Valuation Check After Recent 21.5% Share Price Slide
data.description:
• Wondering if Freshpet at around US$65 per share is priced for opportunity or already reflects its prospects? This article walks through what the current valuation really suggests.
  • The stock recently closed at US$65.03, with a 7 day return of 8.2%, a 30 day return decline of 21.5%, and a 1 year return decline of 21.5%. This makes the recent move particularly important to put in context.
  • Over a longer stretch, Freshpet has a year to date return of 8.1%, a 3 year return of 2.8%, and a 5 year return decline of 61.9%. These figures can pull in very different reactions depending on when you first looked at the stock. These mixed outcomes mean any view on value needs more than a quick glance at the chart.
  • Freshpet currently has a value score of 3 out of 6, reflecting how it screens across multiple checks. The sections ahead will compare several valuation approaches before finishing with a broader framework that can help you read these signals with more confidence.

Find out why Freshpet's -21.5% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Freshpet Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to a present value using a required return.

For Freshpet, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of about $61.99 million, so the focus is on what the cash flows might look like if the business moves into positive territory.

Analyst and extrapolated estimates in this model point to free cash flow reaching about $244.08 million in 2035, with interim projections such as $38.77 million in 2026 and $146 million in 2030, all discounted back to today. When these projected cash flows are added up and adjusted, the model produces an estimated intrinsic value of about $92.94 per share.

Compared with the recent share price of roughly $65, this DCF output implies the stock is about 30.0% undervalued based on these assumptions and projections.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Freshpet Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful gauge because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is currently generating. It helps you see how many dollars investors are willing to pay today for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends largely on growth expectations and risk. Higher expected earnings growth and lower perceived risk usually justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher uncertainty usually point to a lower, more conservative multiple.

Freshpet currently trades on a P/E of 22.93x. That sits above both the Food industry average of 20.39x and the peer average of 18.68x, which on simple comparisons can make the stock look relatively expensive. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Freshpet is 13.33x, which is materially lower than the current P/E.

The Fair Ratio is a proprietary Simply Wall St estimate of what a company’s P/E “should” be, after considering factors like earnings growth, profit margins, industry group, market cap and specific risks. Because it adjusts for these elements, it can give you a more tailored reference point than broad peer or industry averages alone.

Comparing Freshpet’s current P/E of 22.93x with the Fair Ratio of 13.33x suggests the shares are trading above this tailored benchmark.

  • The shift in customer numbers is becoming a key issue for investors tracking Cigna's long term position in health benefits and related services.

Cigna Group, NYSE:CI, recently closed at $277.56, with the share price up 3.8% over the past week and 2.9% over the past month. Over longer periods, returns of 12.6% over three years and 21.8% over five years sit alongside a 1 year decline of 11.4% and a small year to date decline of 0.6%. This mix suggests the market is weighing Cigna's track record against fresh concerns around customer losses.

The gap between revenue growth and a shrinking customer base points to underlying shifts that readers will want to understand. These include how Cigna is pricing products, how it is competing for members, and how it is managing its core health benefits franchise. The following sections break down what this customer trend could mean for NYSE:CI, how it might affect business mix over time, and what questions investors may want to keep on their radar.

The tension for Cigna is that 11.4% annualized revenue growth over five years sits alongside an average 12.1% year on year decline in its customer base over the last two. That suggests the business model is leaning more on higher pricing, product mix and larger accounts than on broad membership growth. For a health benefits player that competes with UnitedHealth, Elevance and CVS Health, shrinking member counts can point to product gaps, tougher renewal cycles or loss of price competitiveness. At the same time, Cigna’s large scale and supplier negotiating leverage still matter in a volume driven industry, especially in areas like pharmacy benefits and specialty drugs. For you as an investor, the key question is whether the customer contraction reflects a deliberate exit from lower margin segments or whether it signals pressure that could eventually weigh on revenue and earnings if it continues.

How This Fits Into The Cigna Group Narrative

  • The reported 12.1% annual decline in customers over two years challenges the narrative assumption that demographic trends will naturally support member growth across Cigna Healthcare.
  • The narrative highlights regulatory and affordability risks around the Evernorth PBM model, but it does not fully address what sustained membership losses could mean for pricing power in future contract negotiations.

  • 🎁 Revenue growth of 11.4% per year over five years shows Cigna has been able to grow the top line despite membership pressure, suggesting some resilience in its core franchises.
  • 🎁 Scale in pharmacy benefits and specialty services may keep Cigna relevant for large employers that want integrated health solutions, even if smaller or lower margin segments shrink.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, keep an eye on whether Cigna can stabilize or improve member trends while maintaining its revenue trajectory. Watch disclosures on where membership is being lost, such as specific employer groups or government related programs, and whether pricing actions lead to further churn. Track how management talks about Evernorth client wins or losses, since PBM contracts can influence both revenue and customer stickiness. data.title: Arista Networks (ANET) Is Up 16.2% After AI Optics Upgrades Spotlight Its Data Center Role
data.description:
• In recent weeks, Arista Networks has drawn attention after several analysts upgraded the stock and highlighted its growing role in AI-focused data center networking, alongside the launch of liquid-cooled, high-density optics modules for large-scale GPU clusters.
  • An interesting angle is how this wave of analyst optimism is rooted not just in AI demand, but in Arista’s push into advanced Ethernet-based optics that could reshape how hyperscalers build and scale their networks.
  • Next, we’ll examine how Arista’s strengthened AI networking leadership, underscored by its new liquid-cooled XPO optics, affects the existing investment narrative.

Arista Networks Investment Narrative Recap

To own Arista, you need to believe that Ethernet-based AI networking can keep gaining share as hyperscalers scale out GPU clusters, and that Arista can stay a preferred vendor despite customer concentration and intense competition. The recent wave of analyst upgrades and raised AI revenue guidance reinforces the near term AI buildout as the key catalyst, while the biggest risk remains any pullback or insourcing by Arista’s largest cloud and AI customers. So far, this news reinforces rather than changes that balance.

The most relevant recent announcement is Arista’s new liquid cooled 12.8 Tbps XPO optics, which targets dense AI data center fabrics. This product directly ties into the AI capex cycle that bullish analysts highlight, potentially deepening Arista’s role in large GPU clusters while also heightening exposure to rapid product cycles and execution risk as customers test new architectures at scale.

Yet investors should also be aware that concentration in a few hyperscale AI customers could quickly magnify any shift in spending or vendor preference...
